37.reason: This else only attaches to the last if, so when the first or second if condition is true but the inner if fails, it incorrectly prints "-". Also, when the first if is true and the inner if is true, the else is skipped, but the second if may also be true, causing duplicate processing. The logic should use else if chains.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  Executes incorrectly due to misplaced else, overriding valid expansions or other type checks.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if uppercase or digit condition fails but lowercase also fails, it prints '-', even if uppercase/digit case was valid. Logic structure is flawed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if the '-' is between uppercase or digit ranges that don't qualify, it will fall through and print '-' correctly, but if it's between lowercase that doesn't qualify, it prints '-', but if it's between uppercase that qualifies, then also checks digits and lowercase, and if lowercase fails, prints '-' again incorrectly. Logic structure is flawed due to separate if blocks instead of else-if chain. Also, no handling for cases where i==0 or i==strlen(s)-1 (i+1 out of bounds).]

14.reason: [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts base twice, causing wrong expansion count.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts base twice, causing wrong expansion count.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts offsets from 'A', which gives same result as direct subtraction but is unnecessarily complex and error-prone. However, the main issue is that this condition does not check if s[i+1] > s[i-1], allowing equal characters which should not expand. Also, missing bounds check for i+1 being within string length.]
